content: I was asleep when the accident happened. I heard a loud crash around 4:20 AM. I didn’t see anything. I’ve never been to the accident site before that night. My husband was a good man, never took risks driving. He had been feeling unwell lately—headaches, dizziness—but I thought it was stress. He was supposed to be at his shop by 8 AM every day. I didn’t know he went out that night. I found out only when I heard the crash. I called the police immediately. I didn’t withdraw any money that morning. The ₹4.2 lakh I took was from our joint account to pay for his funeral and medical bills. I didn’t know about the insurance increase—I only found out about the policy when I was filling the claim. Dr. Mukherjee treated him for back pain. I don’t know anything about forged documents. I swear on my children’s lives that I didn’t harm my husband.
